question_text: A coal-fired power station burns 500 tonnes of coal per day and releases 1,500 tonnes of carbon dioxide (CO2) into the atmosphere. Calculate how much CO2 is released per tonne of coal burned.
correct_answer: 3 tonnes of CO2 per tonne of coal (or 3 kg CO2 per kg coal)
explanation: To find CO2 released per tonne of coal, divide total CO2 by total coal: 1,500 tonnes CO2 ÷ 500 tonnes coal = 3 tonnes CO2 per tonne of coal. This shows how burning fossil fuels produces more mass of CO2 than the fuel itself—this extra carbon comes from oxygen in the air that combines with the fuel.
